name: ANYDESK-BACKDOOR
summary: You should never use malware to infiltrate a target system. With the skill of writing and exploiting technical codes, you can do the best ways of penetration. This is done in order to test and increase the security of the open sourcecode.

documentation.readme: You should never use malware to infiltrate a target system. With the skill of writing and exploiting technical codes, you can do the best ways of penetration. This is done in order to test and increase the security of the open sourcecode.
usage_sections: Features: - Downloads and installs AnyDesk silently. - Sets a password for AnyDesk for added security. - Creates a new user account with administrative privileges. - Hides the new user account from the Windows login screen. - Retrieves the AnyDesk ID for future reference.

Usage: 1. Clone or download this repository to your Windows machine. 2. Open PowerShell with administrator privileges. 3. Navigate to the directory where the script is located. 4. Run the script by executing the following command: 5. Follow the prompts to customize the installation path

arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

use_cases: When you need Command and Control, use ANYDESK-BACKDOOR because You should never use malware to infiltrate a target system. With the skill of writing and exploiting technical codes, you can do the best ways of penetration. T.
When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use ANYDESK-BACKDOOR as a cataloged Command and Control tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Command and Control tools, pair ANYDESK-BACKDOOR with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ANYDESK-BACKDOOR --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
